data.description:

Ju Teng International Holdings Limited ( (HK:3336) ) has shared an update.

Ju Teng International Holdings Limited requested a halt in trading of its shares on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange from 9:00 a.m. on 23 April 2026. The company is led by chairman and CEO Cheng Li-Yu and governed by a board that includes executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors.

The trading halt was imposed pending the release of an announcement relating to Hong Kong’s Code on Takeovers and Mergers and Share Buy-backs, which will contain inside information. The move signals that a potential corporate action involving a takeover, merger or share buy-back is under consideration, and investors are awaiting further clarification on the transaction’s scope and impact.

More about Ju Teng International Holdings Limited

Ju Teng International Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 3336. It is overseen by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, with Cheng Li-Yu serving as chairman and chief executive officer.

data.title: Elegance Optical Faces Ongoing Trading Suspension After HKEX Review
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Elegance Optical International Holdings Limited ( (HK:0907) ) has provided an update.

Elegance Optical International Holdings Limited has been informed that the Listing Review Committee of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange has upheld an earlier decision to suspend trading in its shares, citing the company’s failure to maintain sufficient operations and assets to justify continued listing. The committee found that Elegance Optical’s recently revived eyewear business lacks a proven, coherent plan and sustainable track record, and that its new liquor trading venture is thin on signed contracts, overly reliant on a part-time consultant and unsupported by robust financial data, leaving the company’s asset base and financial position too weak to meet listing requirements.

In its review, the committee highlighted inconsistencies in Elegance Optical’s business model disclosures, questioned the viability of its profit forecasts, and noted that a key shareholder loan was not on normal commercial terms, underscoring concerns over the company’s financial health. As a result, the regulator concluded that, as of the February hearing, Elegance Optical had not demonstrated sufficient operational substance or asset value, prompting the continuation of the trading suspension and signalling heightened risk and uncertainty for shareholders and potential investors.

More about Elegance Optical International Holdings Limited

Elegance Optical International Holdings Limited, incorporated in Bermuda and listed in Hong Kong, historically operates in the eyewear sector and has recently re-focused on its eyewear business while also venturing into a liquor trading business. The company has sought to adopt an asset-light and branded model in eyewear and to build a new revenue stream through liquor trading, but both lines remain at an early stage with limited track record and unclear long-term sustainability.

YTD Price Performance: 12.96%

Average Trading Volume: 2,035,469

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$54.12M

United Company RUSAL has received a requisition from substantial shareholder SUAL Partners to convene an extraordinary general meeting to consider declaring dividends based on the company’s financial performance in the first quarter of 2026. data.description:

Jiangsu Expressway Co ( (HK:0177) ) just unveiled an update.

Jiangsu Expressway has approved a further cash capital injection of RMB128.18 million into its subsidiary Jiangsu Danjin Expressway Co., which is responsible for the Danyang to Jintan section of the Fuli Expressway project. Selected construction contractors for the Danjin project will also join as social capital contributors with RMB458.69 million, lifting Danjin’s registered capital by RMB586.86 million to RMB5.83 billion and slightly diluting Jiangsu Expressway’s stake but strengthening the project’s funding base.

The board confirmed that the capital increase, following a larger RMB2.64 billion pro rata injection earlier in 2025, is neither a related party transaction nor a material asset restructuring under Shanghai and Hong Kong listing rules. The move underscores Jiangsu Expressway’s continued build-out of its toll road portfolio and signals steady progress on a key regional corridor, with implications for long-term traffic growth, earnings contribution, and alignment with local transport development plans.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0177) stock is a Hold with a HK$10.20 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Jiangsu Expressway Co stock, see the HK:0177 Stock Forecast page.

More about Jiangsu Expressway Co

Jiangsu Expressway Company Limited is a PRC-based toll road operator focused on the investment, construction, and operation of expressways and related infrastructure in Jiangsu province. The company generates revenue primarily from toll collections on its expressway network and partners with local government-backed entities to develop regional transportation links.

Legend Holdings Corporation Class H ( (HK:3396) ) just unveiled an update.

Legend Holdings has notified investors that its subsidiary Levima Advanced Materials Corporation has released its unaudited first-quarter 2026 results, prepared under China Accounting Standards and available on the Shenzhen Stock Exchange and CNINFO platforms. The company cautioned that Levima’s figures, while showing strong growth, reflect only one part of Legend Holdings’ broader business and are not directly comparable with the parent’s IFRS-based accounts.

Levima reported a sharp year-on-year improvement in performance for the three months to March 31, 2026, with revenue up 45.93% to RMB 2.25 billion and net profit attributable to shareholders more than doubling to RMB 144.7 million. Operating cash flow surged over sixfold, basic and diluted earnings per share rose to RMB 0.11, and total assets and shareholders’ equity both increased, underscoring a solid start to the year that could bolster the subsidiary’s standing within Legend’s portfolio and in China’s advanced materials sector.

More about Legend Holdings Corporation Class H

Legend Holdings Corporation is a Hong Kong-listed investment holding company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China, with a diversified portfolio that includes Levima Advanced Materials Corporation, a Shenzhen-listed chemicals and materials producer. Levima focuses on advanced materials and related chemical products, and contributes a portion of Legend Holdings’ overall consolidated financial performance.

data.title: FS.COM Downplays Financial Impact of Huawei Trademark Lawsuit
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

FS.COM Limited Class H ( (HK:3355) ) has provided an announcement.

FS.COM Limited has disclosed that it has been served with a civil statement of claim by Huawei Technologies, which alleges that the company has infringed Huawei’s trademark rights and seeks an injunction against further infringement as well as compensation. FS.COM is contesting the allegations, stating that its products do not infringe Huawei’s trademarks and noting that sales of the referenced products contributed less than 0.2% of its 2025 operating revenue.

The company is assessing the case, has engaged legal counsel, and is in active communication with Huawei, while emphasizing that the litigation remains at an early stage. The board said the proceedings have no material adverse impact on the group’s normal operations, financial position, or existing business at present, and pledged to update investors should there be any significant developments in the dispute.

More about FS.COM Limited Class H

FS.COM Limited is a joint stock company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China and listed in Hong Kong. The group provides products and services in the communications and technology sector, with a diversified portfolio such that the disputed product line represents only a small fraction of its overall operating revenue.

Simcere Pharmaceutical Group Limited ( (HK:2096) ) has provided an update.

Simcere Pharmaceutical Group has voluntarily withdrawn and plans to promptly resubmit its New Drug Application in China for rademikibart, an innovative IL-4Rα-targeting monoclonal antibody for atopic dermatitis co-developed with Connect Biopharma, as part of a strategic optimization of its commercialization plan. Phase 3 data showed rapid onset and strong long-term efficacy, with EASI-75, EASI-90 and IGA 0/1 response rates climbing to 96.6%, 85.3% and 87.1% by week 52, underscoring the drug’s potential to strengthen Simcere’s position in the competitive inflammatory and autoimmune therapeutics market and support its broader innovation-led growth strategy.

data.title: Transcenta Reports Strong Preclinical Results for LIV1-Targeting ADCs in Breast and Prostate Cancers
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Transcenta Holding Limited ( (HK:6628) ) has provided an update.

Transcenta Holding Limited has reported new preclinical data for its novel LIV1-targeting antibody-drug conjugate program TST013, including ADC-2 and ADC-3, showing strong anti-tumor activity and favorable tolerability in multiple patient-derived xenograft models. The findings, presented at the 2026 AACR Annual Meeting, underscore differentiated payload-dependent efficacy, with ADC-2 demonstrating robust effects in ER-positive/HER2-negative breast and NSCLC models and ADC-3 achieving sustained tumor suppression in LIV1-high prostate cancer.

The data, alongside earlier results in triple-negative breast cancer, strengthen Transcenta’s case for advancing its LIV1-targeting ADCs into further development for a broad range of LIV1-positive solid tumors. Demonstrated in vivo stability, an extended half-life versus a benchmark analog and a defined maximum tolerated dose for ADC-2 in mice highlight a potentially competitive profile that could enhance the company’s positioning in the emerging ADC oncology segment, while underscoring both the promise and remaining development risks for investors and clinical stakeholders.

More about Transcenta Holding Limited

Transcenta Holding Limited is a biotech company focused on developing antibody-drug conjugates and other biologic therapies targeting solid tumors. Leveraging proprietary antibody platforms and site-specific conjugation technologies, the company is advancing candidates aimed at breast, prostate and lung cancers, with a particular focus on LIV1-positive tumor types.

Average Trading Volume: 1,036,519

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: HK$1.21B

BitStrat Holdings Limited has called its annual general meeting for 29 May 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review the audited consolidated financial statements and the reports of the board and independent auditors for the year ended 31 December 2025. data.title: Sipai Health Technology to Divest Subsidiary Group to Dashenlin for RMB51.73 Million
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from Sipai Health Technology Co., Ltd. ( (HK:0314) ) is now available.

Sipai Health Technology has agreed to sell 100% of the equity in a wholly owned subsidiary, together with the equity of two additional subsidiaries to be injected into that entity before closing, to Dashenlin for RMB51.73 million, subject to a consideration adjustment. Upon completion, the disposed company and its subsidiaries will cease to be part of Sipai’s consolidated group, while Dashenlin will obtain full control of these assets, marking a portfolio realignment that could streamline Sipai’s operations and financial reporting under Hong Kong listing rules.

The deal qualifies as a discloseable transaction under Hong Kong Listing Rules, triggering notification and announcement requirements but not a shareholder vote. As the valuation of the target group involves an income-based assessment of intangible assets and is treated as a profit forecast under local regulations, Sipai plans a further announcement with additional required information, while cautioning investors that the transaction remains conditional and may not ultimately proceed.

More about Sipai Health Technology Co., Ltd.

Sipai Health Technology Co., Ltd. is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating in the healthcare and health technology sector. Through its subsidiaries, it provides medical-related services and solutions, and manages a portfolio of healthcare assets that it actively optimizes via acquisitions and disposals to refine its business focus and capital allocation.

Wai Hung Group Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:3321) ) has provided an announcement.

Zhongke Group Holdings Limited has secured shareholder approval at its extraordinary general meeting on 22 April 2026 for a share consolidation, with every ten existing ordinary shares merged into one consolidated share, following a unanimous poll vote. The meeting, attended by most board members and overseen by an independent scrutineer, reported no voting restrictions, abstentions or opposition among shareholders.

Subject to final listing approval from the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, the share consolidation will take effect on 24 April 2026, with trading in consolidated shares commencing the same day. The company will also change its board lot size on 11 May 2026 from 2,500 existing shares to 10,000 consolidated shares, and will issue new green share certificates, a move aimed at standardising trading units and potentially improving market liquidity and trading efficiency for investors.

More about Wai Hung Group Holdings Ltd.

Zhongke Group Holdings Limited, formerly known as Wai Hung Group Holdings Limited, is a company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 3321. The group operates with a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, and has a substantial shareholder base with 726,696,000 issued shares prior to the latest corporate actions.

data.title: Seres Group Sets Final 2025 Dividend and Details Tax Treatment for H-Share Investors
data.description:

An update from Seres Group Co. Ltd. Class H ( (HK:9927) ) is now available.

Seres Group Co., Ltd. has declared a final ordinary cash dividend of RMB 0.8 per share (HKD 0.913) for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, following shareholder approval on 22 April 2026. The ex-dividend date is set for 6 May 2026, with a record date of 13 May and payment to shareholders scheduled for 5 June 2026.

The company outlined detailed arrangements for Hong Kong share registration, including the latest time to lodge transfer documents on 7 May and a book closure period from 8 to 13 May. It also clarified withholding tax rules for different categories of shareholders, noting a general 10% withholding for non-resident enterprises and individuals, and specifying that mainland corporate investors using Stock Connect must self-declare and pay their income tax on dividends.

More about Seres Group Co. Ltd. Class H

Seres Group Co., Ltd., listed in Hong Kong as a Class H share issuer, operates in mainland China and targets investors through the Shanghai-Hong Kong and Shenzhen-Hong Kong Stock Connect schemes. The company distributes ordinary cash dividends to both resident and non-resident shareholders, with payments subject to differing tax treatments depending on jurisdiction and investor type.
